headline: Retail traders cooling on options and margin - JPMorgan warns on tech
detail: JPMorgan says retail investors are pulling back from options and margin use, and flags this as a specific risk for tech stocks. Your book is ~90% tech on margin, so you're sitting at the exact intersection of this concern. Less retail firepower means less upside momentum for names like PLTR, AVGO, and CRWD.

headline: Gold near $4,000, precious metals rally fading
detail: Safe-haven money rotating into gold and away from risk assets is a backdrop signal worth noting. If that rotation continues, growth and momentum tech names face more selling pressure. Doesn't help your already-red positions.

headline: Amazon adds $48B to India AI and cloud investment
detail: Massive AI and cloud spending commitment from Amazon is a positive signal for the broader semiconductor and infrastructure ecosystem. This is modest indirect support for AVGO and SMH, though it won't move the needle today on your losing positions.

headline: Wall Street coalescing on a single S&P 500 target
detail: When analysts crowd around one consensus number it often marks a sentiment extreme rather than a reliable forecast. Worth keeping in mind as you assess whether the broader market has real upside or is just narrative-driven - directly relevant to your SPY and QQQ hedges holding near breakeven.
